notes: MONOREPO SCOPING: pnpm-workspace.yaml declares frontend/*, services/*, infrastructure/*, scripts — a real workspace split, not a flat repo. react glob is scoped to frontend/ (the only package with .tsx: App.tsx, components/, pages/). db and distributed globs are scoped to services/api/ (the only TS package touching ClickHouse/Redis) — services/graph-analyzer is Python (requirements.txt/pyproject.toml, excluded from this fixture and from any TS glob) and infrastructure/ is config/SQL, not app code. db: services/api/src/config/database.ts instantiates @clickhouse/client's createClient(...) directly — owned analytics DB, not a hosted API. distributed decided HONESTLY, not by dependency presence alone: services/api/src/config/redis.ts by itself is plain key-value cache usage (set/get/del/incr/hset/expire) which per the tag definition does NOT count as a side-effectful boundary. The actual evidence is in services/api/src/services/event.service.ts (included, truncated to 150 lines) — processUIEvent/processPerformanceMetric/processABTestResult each call this.publishEvent(...) which does this.redis.publish('events', ...) (verbatim excerpt now included in the fixture file, source lines 531-539, alongside publishRealtimeEvent at 392-398), plus event.service.ts:396 publishRealtimeEvent publishes to a realtime channel that services/api/src/routes/events.ts:34 and src/app.ts:198 consume via a live '/ws' websocket route (@fastify/websocket, grepped, not included as a separate file to stay under the file cap). This is genuine cross-process pub/sub fan-out (ingest process -> Redis channel -> websocket-connected browser clients), which the tag definition's 'queue/pubsub between processes' clause covers — not a plain cache read/write. frontend/src/services/websocket.service.ts (not included) is the frontend-side consumer of that same channel; not needed as separate evidence since the backend side already proves the boundary. react: frontend uses react-router-dom, JSX components under frontend/src/components and frontend/src/pages (Layout.tsx included, 48 lines, uses react-router-dom's useLocation/Link). No test files exist anywhere in the repo (grepped *.test.*/*.spec.* across the whole tree, zero hits, despite both frontend and services/api package.json declaring a vitest "test" script) so the test glob is left at corpus default, unverified against a real convention — flagging this as a genuine gap for human review, since vitest is configured but apparently unused.

rules_notes.keys-are-identity-not-position: + frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx — JSX list render navigation.map with key= — the keyed-list surface
rules_notes.external-call-timeout: + services/api/src/config/database.ts — outbound ClickHouse/Redis clients — the out-of-process call-site surface (timeouts currently set, subject present)
rules_notes.serialized-data-outlives-its-writer: + services/api/src/services/event.service.ts — publishEvent JSON.stringifies messages onto Redis channel consumed by other processes; persisted ClickHouse schemas
rules_notes.cross-boundary-facts-are-queryable: + services/api/src/services/event.service.ts — redis.publish and ClickHouse inserts cross process boundaries
rules_notes.remote-call-has-a-third-outcome: + services/api/src/services/event.service.ts — processUIEvent et al await network-boundary inserts/publishes returning Promise<string>
rules_notes.mood-names-commands-facts: + services/api/src/services/event.service.ts — publishEvent tags messages 'ui_event'/'performance_metric' on events channels — event names exist
rules_notes.replicated-state-is-a-deterministic-fold: - events appended to ClickHouse, never folded into a rebuilt replica
rules_notes.surface-write-conflict-never-silently-lw: - append-only inserts and plain Redis cache set/get; no conflict-resolution surface
rules_notes.restart-policy-as-data: - Redis is pub/sub fan-out and caching, not a supervised work queue
